engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Outlander Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Outlander across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Mitsubishi's core Outlander diesel, introduced 2006; common-rail DI-D tech brought efficiency but EGR/turbo vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: 2 DI-D, 2.2 DI-D, 3 DI-D, 4 DI-D, ES DI-D, SE DI-D, LE DI-D, XLS DI-D, GT DI-D, Diamond DI-D
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking and carbon buildup (60k-90k miles)
Turbo actuator sticking and boost loss (70k-100k miles)
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starts
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4N14 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Early second-gen Outlander diesel; PSA-sourced unit replaced by 4N14 for improved refinement.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 DI-D, SE DI-D, LE DI-D (ZG/ZH chassis)
commonFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey use
EGR valve sticking causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4D68 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: First-gen MIVEC petrol for Outlander; lightweight aluminium block but timing chain tensioner concerns emerged.
compatibleVariants: ES, SE, LE, XLS, 2.0, 2.0 MIVEC, PHEV (base petrol unit)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle (70k-100k miles)
Oil consumption from valve stem seals (80k+ miles)
Water pump failure leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4B11 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Larger-displacement MIVEC unit offering more torque; shared architecture with 4B11 but improved low-end response.
compatibleVariants: SE, LE, XLS, GT, 2.4, 2.4 MIVEC, Diamond, PHEV (base petrol unit)
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear causing noise (80k-110k miles)
Water pump seal failure and coolant loss
MIVEC solenoid clogging affecting variable timing
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4B12 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L/2.4L Petrol (SOHC)
entries.history: Updated SOHC MIVEC variants for third-gen Outlander; simplified design improved reliability but retained core architecture.
compatibleVariants: 2.0, 2.4, ES, SE, LE, XLS, GT (ZK/ZM chassis)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (similar to 4B series)
Oil pump pressure loss at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4J11/4J12 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Outlander's performance flagship engine; SOHC MIVEC V6 delivering smooth power but complex servicing requirements.
compatibleVariants: GT, GT S, 3.0 V6, Diamond V6 (ZG/ZH/ZK chassis)
commonFailures: Oil pump drive chain wear causing low pressure
Timing chain tensioner failure on high-mileage units
Valve cover gasket leaks leading to oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6B31 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol MHEV
entries.history: Nissan-sourced 2.5L four-cylinder with mild-hybrid assist; latest Outlander powertrain focusing on efficiency.
commonFailures: Mild-hybrid battery interaction faults (early units)
CVT transmission compatibility calibration issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for PR25DD Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Hybrid
entries.history: Outlander PHEV's series-parallel hybrid system; petrol engine works with twin electric motors for AWD capability.
compatibleVariants: GT PHEV, GT PHEV S, PHEV, PHEV S, Diamond PHEV
commonFailures: Inverter cooling system faults causing reduced power
Generator motor bearing wear at high mileage
Hybrid battery thermal management issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4B11-PHEV Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Outlander Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Outlander's 4N14 diesel and 4B11/4B12 petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with EGR cooler failures, timing chain tensioner wear, and turbo actuator faults driving urgent searches. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Outlander 4N14 EGR Cooler Failure - Why Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup and thermal stress crack the EGR cooler core, causing coolant loss and white smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler, clean EGR valve, reset adaptations
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi Outlander worth £4,000-£8,000, a rebuilt 4N14 with upgraded EGR components offers better long-term value than repeated cooler replacements. We recommend replacement if the engine has exceeded 80k miles or shows additional wear signs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4N14 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Outlander

problems.group: Mitsubishi Outlander Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Mitsubishi Outlander Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why 4B11/4B12 Petrol Engines Rattle
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure, allowing chain slap that damages guides and can jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and guides before chain damage
Chain, tensioner, guides, water pump, seals
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing system

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start but the engine still runs smoothly, a preventative timing kit replacement offers good value. If the chain has jumped or caused valve damage, replacement is the only viable option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4B11/4B12 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Outlander Turbo Actuator Fault - Why 4N14 Diesel Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup jams the turbo wastegate actuator, causing over/under-boost and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace actuator, reset ECU adaptations
Rebuild turbo with new bearings, seals, actuator
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo

problems.recommendation: If boost loss is intermittent and the turbo sounds healthy, actuator replacement is cost-effective. If there's shaft play or oil leakage, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo offers better long-term security.
problems.cta: Compare 4N14 turbo diesel engine prices for your Mitsubishi Outlander

problems.group: Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV Inverter Cooling Fault
problems.h4: Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV Inverter Cooling Fault - Why Hybrid Systems Fail
problems.rootCause: Coolant leak or pump failure in the inverter cooling circuit causes overheating and system shutdown.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump, hoses, refill coolant, reset system
Replace inverter unit, recalibrate hybrid system
Supply & fit rebuilt petrol engine + hybrid system check

problems.recommendation: PHEV systems require specialist diagnosis. If the inverter is undamaged, cooling system repair is viable. For high-mileage PHEVs or repeated faults, a rebuilt powertrain with hybrid system validation offers peace of mind.
problems.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV engine replacement quotes

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Outlander Oil Pump Failure - Why Low Oil Pressure Destroys Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Outlander 3.0L V6, 2.4L Petrol (2006-2017, 6B31/4B12 engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Oil pump drive chain wear reduces pressure, causing bearing wear and eventual engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump and drive chain before bearing damage
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-50k miles if caught on warning light

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded oil system

problems.recommendation: Oil pressure warnings require immediate attention. If caught early, pump replacement may save the engine. If bearing knock is present, replacement is the only option to avoid write-off.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 6B31/4B12 Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Outlander
finalCta.paragraph: From EGR cooler cracks to timing chain rattles, Mitsubishi Outlander engine problems are predictable and solvable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Outlander specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Outlander and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Outlander Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Outlander is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L diesel was offered in early second-generation Outlanders (ZG/ZH), using PSA-sourced engines before Mitsubishi's own 4N14 became standard. These units are less common today but remain viable for budget replacements.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve sticking causing rough idle
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Outlander 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L 4N14 DI-D is the Outlander's core diesel engine across all generations, dominating UK replacement searches due to high fleet usage and EGR/turbo failure patterns.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Outlander 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L 4B11 MIVEC powered entry-level Outlanders and serves as the petrol base unit for PHEV variants. Timing chain tensioner wear is the most common replacement driver.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L 4B12/4J12 MIVEC offered more torque for heavier Outlander variants. Shared architecture with 2.0L but improved low-end response; water pump and timing guide wear are common.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander 3.0L V6 Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L 6B31 V6 was the Outlander's performance flagship, offering smooth power but complex servicing. Oil pump drive chain wear is the primary failure concern for replacement.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander 2.5L Petrol MHEV Engines
items.description: The latest 2.5L PR25DD with mild-hybrid assist powers fourth-generation Outlanders. As a newer powertrain, replacement data is limited but CVT compatibility is critical.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander 2.5L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mitsubishi Outlander Hybrid / Electrified Engine Sizes

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV Hybrid Engines
items.description: The Outlander PHEV combines a 2.0L petrol engine with twin electric motors for AWD capability. Inverter cooling faults and generator bearing wear drive most replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mitsubishi Outlander Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L 4N14 DI-D diesel dominates Outlander replacement searches across all generations, with EGR and turbo failures driving urgent enquiries from fleet and private owners.
items.descriptor: The 2.2L 4N14 DI-D diesel dominates Outlander replacement searches across all generations, with EGR and turbo failures driving urgent enquiries from fleet and private owners.

Turbo actuator sticking causing boost loss and limp mode
High UK replacement demand on ex-fleet and lease vehicles

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Outlander Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Outlander's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The second-generation Outlander launched with new 4B11/4B12 petrol and 4N14 diesel engines, replacing first-gen units and establishing the platform for a decade.
keyChanges: New ZG/ZH platform with improved crash safety and interior space
Introduction of 4B11 2.0L and 4B12 2.4L MIVEC petrol engines
4N14 2.2L DI-D diesel becomes core powertrain for UK market
First availability of 6B31 3.0L V6 in GT flagship trim
knownFor: Robust 4N14 diesel block but EGR cooler vulnerabilities emerge
4B11 timing chain tensioner wear becomes known issue post-80k miles
Strong residual values and high UK registration numbers
First generation to offer factory-fitted satellite navigation
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2012 Mitsubishi Outlander replacement engines
years.ticker: Outlander 2.2 DI-D engine replacement • Outlander 4B11 timing chain fix • Outlander 4N14 EGR cooler cost

years.description: The ZK-generation Outlander introduced refined 4N14 diesel and updated 4J11/4J12 petrol engines, plus the game-changing PHEV hybrid variant in 2014.
keyChanges: New ZK platform with lighter body and improved fuel economy
Introduction of PHEV variant with 4B11-PHEV hybrid powertrain (2014)
Updated 4J11/4J12 SOHC MIVEC petrol engines for improved reliability
Euro 5/6 emissions compliance across diesel range
knownFor: PHEV becomes UK's best-selling plug-in hybrid, driving specialist enquiries
4N14 turbo actuator faults emerge as common high-mileage issue
Improved interior quality and standard AWD on most variants
Strong demand for rebuilt PHEV powertrains from private owners
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2017 Mitsubishi Outlander replacement engines
years.ticker: Outlander PHEV engine replacement • Outlander 4N14 turbo actuator fix • Outlander 2.4 petrol water pump cost

years.description: The facelifted ZK and early ZM Outlanders received Euro 6d-temp compliant 4N14 diesel and updated infotainment, with PHEV range extended.
keyChanges: Euro 6d-temp emissions compliance for 4N14 diesel engines
PHEV battery capacity increased to 13.8kWh (2019), extending electric range
Updated front styling and standard smartphone integration
Final production year for non-PHEV ZK variants (2021)
knownFor: PHEV remains top choice for company car drivers seeking tax efficiency
Late-model 4N14 units show improved EGR cooler durability
Strong used market demand keeps replacement engine enquiries high
Final ZK production year creates parts availability concerns for some trims
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2021 Mitsubishi Outlander replacement engines
years.ticker: Outlander PHEV inverter fault • Outlander Euro 6 diesel engine • Outlander 2.4 petrol timing chain

years.description: The all-new ZM-generation Outlander introduced Nissan-sourced PR25DD 2.5L MHEV petrol and updated PHEV architecture, marking a significant powertrain shift.
keyChanges: New ZM platform co-developed with Nissan, sharing Qashqai/X-Trail architecture
Introduction of PR25DD 2.5L MHEV petrol as standard non-hybrid option
Updated PHEV system with larger battery and improved electric range
Discontinuation of pure diesel variants in UK market
knownFor: New powertrains mean limited replacement data; early reliability monitoring ongoing
PHEV remains strong seller with improved electric-only capability
CVT transmission standard across all variants, requiring calibration expertise
Specialist suppliers adapting to new engine codes and hybrid architectures
years.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Outlander 2021+ replacement engine availability

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Outlander Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Outlander engines typically cost £1,800-£4,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel 4N14 units sit at £1,950-£3,400, while PHEV powertrains reach £2,800-£4,200 due to hybrid complexity.
keyPoints: 4B11/4B12 petrol rebuilt: £1,800-£2,800 supply-only
4N14 diesel rebuilt: £1,950-£3,400 including upgraded EGR components
PHEV hybrid powertrain: £2,800-£4,200 with inverter system check
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on garage location
All prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi Outlander engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes fit the Mitsubishi Outlander?
items.answer: UK Mitsubishi Outlanders use 4B11/4B12/4J11/4J12 petrol, 4N14/4D68 diesel, 6B31 V6, and 4B11-PHEV hybrid codes. Your registration or VIN confirms exact compatibility.
keyPoints: Diesel: 4N14 (2.2L core), 4D68 (early 2.0L)
Hybrid: 4B11-PHEV (PHEV variants only)
Use our reg checker to verify exact code for your chassis
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Outlander engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: How long does Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines dispatch in 3-5 working days; supply & fit bookings typically complete in 1-2 days once the engine arrives at your chosen garage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines: 3-5 working days dispatch from specialist
Used engines: Often available for next-day collection
Supply & fit: 1-2 days garage time plus engine delivery
PHEV/hybrid: Allow extra day for system calibration
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Outlander engine supply & fit availability near you

items.question: Is a reconditioned Mitsubishi Outlander engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes - professionally reconditioned Mitsubishi Outlander engines with upgraded failure-prone components (EGR, timing chain, turbo) and 12-month warranty offer excellent reliability for high-mileage SUVs.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units address known 4N14/4B11 failure patterns
Bench-tested with compression and pressure verification
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Choose suppliers offering upgraded EGR/timing components
items.cta: View reconditioned Mitsubishi Outlander engine options with warranty

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Mitsubishi Outlander?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but require ECU reprogramming, transmission compatibility checks, and emissions compliance verification - we recommend matching original code for hassle-free replacement.
keyPoints: 4B11 and 4J11 share architecture but need ECU adaptation
Diesel-to-petrol swaps require extensive wiring and emissions work
PHEV powertrains cannot be substituted with standard engines
Our specialists confirm compatibility before dispatch
items.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Outlander engine swap compatibility

items.question: Does Mitsubishi Outlander engine replacement affect warranty?
items.answer: Replacing your Outlander engine with a rebuilt unit from our network maintains a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on the engine; remaining manufacturer warranty on other components is unaffected.
keyPoints: Engine warranty is separate from vehicle manufacturer warranty
12-month unlimited mileage coverage standard on rebuilt units
Keep installation receipts for warranty validation
Hybrid/PHEV systems require specialist calibration for warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: What is the difference between used, reconditioned and remanufactured Outlander engines?
items.answer: Used engines are second-hand with variable history; reconditioned units are inspected and repaired addressing common failures; remanufactured engines are stripped and rebuilt to factory spec with new components throughout.
keyPoints: Used: Lowest cost, verify compression test and mileage history
Reconditioned: Mid-price, addresses known failure points like EGR/timing
Remanufactured: Premium option, OEM-spec rebuild with extended warranty
All options include 12-month warranty when sourced via EngineMarket
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Outlander engine types and pricing options

items.question: How do I know which Mitsubishi Outlander engine I need?
items.answer: Enter your registration above for instant DVLA verification of your exact engine code, fuel type and variant - eliminating guesswork and ensuring perfect compatibility for your replacement.
keyPoints: Engine code is stamped on cylinder block or valve cover
V5 registration document lists engine capacity and fuel type
Our reg checker cross-references DVLA and specialist databases
PHEV variants require specific hybrid powertrain confirmation
items.cta: Identify your Mitsubishi Outlander engine code instantly with reg check

items.question: Do you supply Mitsubishi Outlander engines with fitting?
items.answer: Yes - our network includes UK garages offering supply & fit service for Mitsubishi Outlander engines, including ECU coding, fluid refill and post-install road test.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £450-£850 to engine price depending on location
Garages experienced with Outlander CVT and hybrid system calibration
Single-point warranty covering engine and labour available
Mobile fitting options in some regions for added convenience
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi Outlander engine supply & fit service nationwide

items.answer: Every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Mitsubishi Outlander engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.

Coverage includes internal components and upgraded failure-prone parts

Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before order confirmation
